senses.gloss: verbal noun of مَاتَ (māta, “to die”) (form I)

examples.text: أَيۡنَمَا تَكُونُوا۟ يُدۡرِككُّمُ ٱلۡمَوۡتُ وَلَوۡ كُنتُمۡ فِي بُرُوجࣲ مُّشَيَّدَةࣲ
examples.english: Wherever you may be, death will overtake you, even if you should be within towers of lofty construction.

senses.gloss: to make die, let perish, to kill, to cause the death of

etymology.text: Inherited from Proto-Semitic *mawt- (“death”). Compare Akkadian 𒁁 (mūtum, “death”), Ugaritic 𐎎𐎚 (mt, “death”), Aramaic ܡܘܬܐ (mawtā), Hebrew מָוֶת (mávet, “death”), Amharic ሞት (mot, “death”) and Egyptian mtt:m*A14
